chosen: It's finally time to introduce Kerrigan Robotics to the world! After a year of keeping things under wraps while we developed, we're super excited and ready for our first public announcement. I'm thrilled to announce that Sanjay Lal is joining as Co-Founder & CEO, while I step into the Co-Founder & CTO role. This is the right leader at exactly the right moment, and a milestone that reflects the incredible scale we have reached based on the work our team has put in, including engineers like Christian Cassar and Bill Rose, who've been with us from the start. As I continue to look at manufacturing, there is still a massive Physical AI Deployment Gap. It’s trapped in "pilot purgatory" because most systems can't orchestrate and reason across the grit, heterogeneous complexity, and 24/7 pressure of a real production line. We are building the agentic AI nervous system for manufacturing - a unified layer that manages the entire automation lifecycle from design to 24/7 operations. Built on the pedigree of orchestrating thousands of robots at Tesla, we are now bringing that same industrial intelligence to our customers in the semiconductor, F&B, and automotive sectors - and we’re just getting started. I am honored to co-lead this team of automation veterans, and I’m grateful for the partnership of our investors at Transition Ventures (David Helgason & Mona Alsubaei), Overture Ventures (Shomik Dutta, Brandon Hurlbut & Leila Pirbay), Foothill Ventures (Eric Rosenblum & Theresa (Wei) Dai), and WovenEarth Ventures (Jane Woodward) as we scale this vision. The future of manufacturing isn't just about more robots - it’s about Industrial Reasoning.
rejected: Tomorrow is a very important day for Product School, and I keep thinking about something Robby Stein said on our podcast: "As a founder, as a leader, everyone needs to think, what's gonna be the most important thing that if we get it right, this is potentially a five or 10 year opportunity of growth, of value, of helpfulness that we can give people." Tomorrow, we're launching something big, something that – we hope – can help raise a new generation of AI-native product managers. The role of a PM now comes with a lot of new expectations. We have been tirelessly releasing brand-new AI-first courses as well as updating our original certifications to account for the new skills necessary. With this new launch, we want to make learning these crucial new skills more accessible – so the industry transformation goes faster and smoother. More details tomorrow

chosen: I’ve spent the last decade at the heart of the software and AI transformation of automotive. We’ve seen how Autonomous Driving and SDVs redefined what a car can be, but as I look at manufacturing, there is still a massive Physical AI Deployment Gap. It’s trapped in "pilot purgatory" because most systems can't orchestrate and reason across the grit, heterogeneous complexity, and 24/7 pressure of a real production line. That’s why I’m thrilled to join Kerrigan Robotics as Co-Founder & CEO, and I'm deeply humbled to lead the effort Mohamad Fayez Taha (Co-Founder & CTO), Christian Cassar and Bill Rose started last year. They’ve built something truly special, and I’m excited to help them scale it. We are building the agentic AI nervous system for manufacturing - a unified layer that manages the entire automation lifecycle from design to 24/7 operations. Built on the pedigree of orchestrating thousands of robots at Tesla, we are now bringing that same industrial intelligence to our customers in the semiconductor, F&B, and automotive sectors - and we’re just getting started. I am honored to lead this team of automation veterans, and I’m grateful for the partnership of our investors at Transition Ventures, Overture Ventures, Foothill Ventures, and WovenEarth Ventures as we scale this vision. The future of manufacturing isn't just about more robots - it’s about Industrial Reasoning.
rejected: Over the past few years at Canva, one thing has become very clear to me: Great operations is the ultimate force multiplier. ✨ As our Marketing team has grown to 450+ people across Brand, Creative, Performance, International, MarTech, Research & Data, Product Marketing and more -- the difference between momentum and noise often comes down to how well we run. The right operator doesn’t just keep things moving. They make the whole system better. That’s why I’m hiring an Executive Operations Partner to join my core team, working directly alongside me and Ally Garrett, our Chief of Staff for Marketing. This role sits at the centre of our marketing engine and operates across two core domains: 1️⃣ CMO Operations Architecting how I operate to best deliver for Canva and the marketing team -- strategically and sustainably. Not just calendar management. Owning the full operating rhythm that drives clarity, pace and focus across Marketing. This includes: • Designing and running a high-leverage weekly & monthly cadence • Sequencing global priorities across time zones • Triage, trade-offs and proactive problem solving • Preparing briefs for leadership, board and external moments • Ensuring follow-through and accountability • Owning complex international travel & logistics • Reducing friction and protecting energy so I can focus on what matters most 2️⃣ Key Programs & Organisational Effectiveness Owning high-impact, cross-functional initiatives that shape how Marketing runs at scale. This includes: • Turning ambiguity into clear, trackable plans • Driving alignment across global teams • Raising risks early and proposing solutions • Building scalable workflows and systems • Powering our biggest moments — Cannes, Canva Create, Droptober, global offsites and major launches The goal: help our global marketing org operate with clarity, velocity and intent. I’ve seen how powerful this role can be. ➡️ Junia Ooi joined as one of our first operations hires — today she runs our LATAM business. ➡️ Ally Garrett scaled our global marketing operations and program management function — now leading a high powered ops group supporting our Consumer, B2B, Comms and Growth teams. ➡️ Veronica Tebbitt drove tremendous impact in a similar role and went on to support our CEO directly. This isn’t just a support role. It really is a launchpad. If you’re an exceptional operator who: • Thrives in complexity • Has strong judgement and isn’t afraid to push back • Gets energy from building systems that unlock teams • Is comfortable supporting C-suite leaders at pace • Already uses AI to work smarter • Has real ambition and gas in the tank I’d love to hear from you -- or from someone you rate highly!

chosen: Spent the day at Y Combinator's W26 Demo Day in San Francisco, and a few things stood out from Garry Tan’s opening remarks: • ~90% of the batch is AI • Solo founders can now build faster than entire teams could just a few years ago • Garry said he's built more software this year than in all of 2013 when he founded Posthaven and expects 20× that output over the rest of 2026 • Companies in the batch are averaging ~14% weekly revenue growth • 14 companies are already at $1M+ ARR • 23,000 applications were received, with <1% accepted • ~70% of the batch applied with zero revenue My big takeaway: AI isn’t just another category in this batch; it’s foundational for building today. Also, fun seeing prolific YC startup investor Joe Montana join one company’s pitch on stage. And great to see so many fellow Pioneer Fund investors, including Russell Smith and Taro Fukuyama, as well. The pace founders can move at right now is mind blowing, and the future is bright.

chosen: We are in the manifesto porn era of AI. I think I'm here for it. Seems like every week there's a new 20-page prophecy delivered in the same Sarah Connor-style fluent apocal-ish. What may have started with decrees from the high priests of foundation model companies has spilled over to the ranks of hedge fund quants and engineering middle management. I thought AI would kill the art of articulation, but it turns out maybe it bestows all of us with creative writing degrees and minors in Slack channel stimulation. Today, I discovered this fascinating one from Geoff McDonald, tl;dr — malicious AI worms will completely reimagine threats to cybersecurity. Something for everybody here, but my takeaway: we have a lot of work to do in consumer security right now...here's why: + 25 years ago, the cybersecurity market was <$10B, split roughly 60% enterprise and 40% consumer. + Today, the market is ~$250B, split 90% enterprise and 10% consumer, and enterprise cybersecurity startups capture >95% of new venture capital. There used to be a time when consumer security companies led the way. Firms like McAfee and Norton pioneered threat research and antivirus innovation. Now, that work is done for large enterprises, while consumers wait longer and hope it trickles down to improve their protection. Somewhere along the way, we lost some innovation muscle in protecting the digital daily lives of everyday people and families. We started Savi Security because we need to get busy building it back.
rejected: Today, we’re killing 2,000 integration companies. Including our own. After 12 years of work, we’re open-sourcing the world’s largest integrations knowledge base for AI agents: → connects any AI agent to 250+ apps → plugs it with 47,000+ actions → each API endpoint and auth flow is verified → battle-tested by 17,000 customers We’re making everything open source. Our former company, Pica, is now One. One lets you connect and monitor your AI agents from one place. So instead of stitching together brittle integrations, expired OAuth tokens, rate limits, broken permissions, and random API docs… your agents just work. Here’s what that looks like: → One CLI command to connect to hundreds of apps → precise permissions, so agents can’t go rogue → One Auth Link, so users can share app access without handing over API keys → One Flow, so your agent can run deterministic automations for almost no cost Knowledge should be free. Using it should be affordable. So comment "One" below and repost to get: → UNLIMITED connections → 1M API requests FREE every month → 47,000 integration actions

chosen: Over the past two months I've interviewed more than a dozen founders from breakout AI-native companies. What they do differently ⤵️ Some of the companies already scaled to $100M ARR and beyond – including Clay, Gamma, HeyGen, Intercom (Fin.ai), and Fireworks AI. Others are fast-growing AI-native startups who are well-positioned to join them including 7AI, AirOps, bolt.new, Fyxer, GC AI, GrowthX AI, HappyRobot, and incident.io. Read the full post in today's Growth Unhinged: https://lnkd.in/eMGzZRbx Here’s a preview of some of the more interesting takeaways: 1. Finding PMF in SaaS was an artisanal endeavor; it was usually careful, steady, gradual, and incremental. For AI-native startups PMF was often obvious and immediate. 2. AI-native companies reached $1 million ARR twice as fast as B2B SaaS. It took the average company I interviewed only 12 months (!) from when they started building & 7 months from having a usable live product. 3. Price sensitivity drops dramatically when AI does real work. Gamma A/B tested pricing and found that people were willing to pay double the price of a conventional productivity app. The highest price they tested ($20 per month) performed best. 4. Product success hinges on AI performance crossing a reliability threshold. 5. Explicit user feedback (thumbs up/down) can be surprisingly insightful on its own. 6. SaaS companies usually hired their first AEs as they approached $1 million ARR. AI companies tend to delay hiring their first AE until about $2-5M ARR. GrowthX AI reached $8M ARR purely through founder-led sales. 6. The winning GTM motions combine bottom-up discovery with top-down sales. Interestingly, half of the AI-native companies I interviewed started with a primarily self-serve GTM. Only one is still primarily focused on self-serve today. 7. The best GTM teams look more like consulting firms than SaaS sellers. Their sales teams aren’t selling features; they’re selling a business transformation powered by AI. --- 🙏 HUGE thank you to those who participated: Varun Anand (Clay), Jon Noronha (Gamma), Joshua X. (HeyGen), Des Traynor (Intercom, Fin.ai), Lin Qiao (Fireworks AI), Archie Hollingsworth (Fyxer), Lior Div (7AI), Matt Hammel (AirOps), Alexander Berger (bolt.new), Cecilia Ziniti (GC AI), Marcel Santilli (GrowthX), Pablo Palafox (HappyRobot), and Stephen Whitworth (incident.io). --- Today's newsletter is supported by AirOps who just released a fantastic new AI search playbook for 2026. I'll drop the link in the comments.
rejected: AI Changed How I Am Building My Next Roll Up 🔝 Two years ago, sourcing an acquisition target meant hiring a broker, paying $10-20K for a CIM, and spending weeks on manual research. Today I wake up at 4am and my AI stack does what used to take a team of 3: My problem is I now have too much deal flow. → Perplexity scans industries and pulls market data in minutes → Claude Dispatch runs my outreach and research workflows simultaneously → Claude Cowork builds the analysis docs, financial models, and diligence frameworks My Opex to get started on a deal went from five figures to a nominal cost. →The barrier to acquiring businesses has drastically changed. →You don't need a fund. →You don't need a team of analysts. →You need a framework, AI tools, and the discipline to wake up early and put in the work before anyone else is online. The people who figure this out in 2026 will build portfolios that took the last generation a decade to assemble. Subscribe to my newsletter on the latest ways to build a platform roll up today.
